tagline: Local-first password manager with AES encryption

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Welcome to Password Vault. Your privacy is important to us. This Privacy Policy explains how our app handles your information, including what data we collect, how we use it, and your rights regarding that data.

subsections.subtitle: Personal Data
subsections.content: We do not collect, store, or share any personal data such as your name, email address, or phone number.

subsections.subtitle: Passwords and Sensitive Information
subsections.content: All passwords, usernames, notes, and related information you store in Password Vault are saved locally on your device. This data is encrypted and never uploaded to our servers.

subsections.subtitle: Usage Data
subsections.content: We may collect anonymous usage information such as app performance, error logs, and crash reports to improve the quality and stability of the app.

sections.title: How We Use Information
list: To ensure secure storage of your passwords locally on your device.
To provide features such as password strength checking and vault management.
To analyze app performance and fix bugs or crashes.

sections.title: Data Security
sections.content: Your data security is our priority. Password Vault uses strong encryption methods to protect your saved information. Since the data never leaves your device, you are in full control of your information.

sections.title: Data Sharing
sections.content: We do not share, sell, or transmit your personal information or stored passwords to any third parties.

sections.title: Third-Party Services
sections.content: Password Vault may use third-party libraries such as Firebase for crash reporting. These services collect only non-identifiable information to help improve app stability.

sections.title: Your Rights
sections.content: Since all your data is stored locally on your device, you may manage, delete, or back up your information at any time through the app's settings.

sections.title: Changes to This Privacy Policy
sections.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will be posted within the app and on this page with an updated effective date.

sections.title: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy or the app, please contact us at:

tagline: Spiritual jaap tracker with aartis and chalisa collection

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Welcome to Japnaam. Your privacy is important to us. This Privacy Policy explains how our app manages your data, including what information we collect, how we use it, and your rights regarding that information.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.content: Japnaam is designed to help you track your jaap (chant counts), access a wide list of aartis, and manage your spiritual practices. We do not collect or store any personal information such as your name, email, or phone number. All your jaap counts, preferences, and notes are stored locally on your device. This information is never uploaded to our servers.

sections.title: Usage Data
sections.content: We may collect anonymous usage information such as crash reports and app performance data. This is done only to improve the stability and experience of the app. No personal or sensitive information is collected in this process.

sections.title: How We Use Information
list: Track and display your jaap counts.
Provide you access to aartis and related spiritual content.
Enhance your app experience with personalized features.
Fix bugs and improve app performance through crash reports.

sections.title: Data Security
sections.content: We prioritize your privacy and data security. All jaap counts and related data are stored locally, ensuring that you remain in full control of your information. Since the app does not require internet to function, your data stays private.

sections.title: Data Sharing
sections.content: Japnaam does not share, sell, or transmit your personal information or jaap counts to any third parties.

sections.title: Third-Party Services
sections.content: The app may use third-party libraries, such as Firebase, for crash reporting. These services collect only non-identifiable information necessary for improving the app's stability.

sections.title: Changes to This Privacy Policy
sections.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Updates will be reflected in the app and on this page with a revised effective date.

sections.title: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy or the app, please contact us at:

tagline: Free, 100% local screen recorder with no accounts or cloud storage

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Welcome to OpenRec. Your privacy is important to us. This Privacy Policy explains how our extension handles your information, including what data we collect, how we use it, and your rights regarding that data.

subsections.subtitle: Personal Data
subsections.content: We do not collect, store, or share any personal data such as your name, email address, or phone number. OpenRec has no accounts, no sign-up, and no login of any kind.

subsections.subtitle: Recordings and Related Content
subsections.content: Every screen recording, webcam clip, thumbnail, and GIF you create is saved locally in your browser's IndexedDB storage, on your own device. This content is never uploaded, transmitted, or made accessible to us or any third party.

subsections.subtitle: Preferences and Settings
subsections.content: Your chosen recording quality, theme, accent color, webcam bubble style, capture mode, and similar preferences are saved locally using the browser's chrome.storage.local API, solely so your settings persist between sessions.

subsections.subtitle: Usage Data
subsections.content: We do not collect any usage analytics, telemetry, error logs, or crash reports. OpenRec makes no network requests of any kind and has no server to send such data to, even if it wanted to.

sections.title: How We Use Your Information
sections.content: Because everything OpenRec handles stays on your device, there is no server-side processing or use of your data by us. Your recordings and preferences are used only by the extension itself, running locally in your browser, to display your library, play back recordings, and apply the settings you chose.

sections.title: Data Storage and Security
sections.content: Recordings, thumbnails, and library metadata are stored in your browser's local IndexedDB; preferences are stored via chrome.storage.local. Both are sandboxed to the OpenRec extension and this browser profile — they are not synced to any account or server we control. Deleting a recording moves it to the in-app Bin first, and permanently deleting it (or emptying the Bin) removes it from IndexedDB. Uninstalling the extension, or clearing your browser's extension data, permanently erases everything OpenRec has stored.

sections.title: Backups and File Exports
sections.content: OpenRec lets you export your recordings and settings into a single backup file, or export an individual recording or GIF. These files are generated entirely on your device and saved wherever you choose using your browser's normal download/file picker. We never see, receive, or store a copy of any file you export or import.

subsections.subtitle: Storage
subsections.content: Used only to save your app preferences locally on your device, as described above. No personal data is stored through this permission.

subsections.subtitle: Screen, Microphone, and Camera Access
subsections.content: These are not extension permissions but standard browser prompts (getDisplayMedia and getUserMedia), shown by Chrome itself only when you click Start recording, exactly as any website could request them. You choose what to share each time, and access ends when your recording session ends.

sections.title: Data Sharing and Third Parties
sections.content: OpenRec does not share, sell, rent, or otherwise disclose your data to any third party, because it does not transmit your data anywhere in the first place. There are no advertising networks, analytics SDKs, or third-party services integrated into the extension.

sections.title: No Remote Code
sections.content: All code that runs in OpenRec — its interface, video player, trimming, and GIF export — is bundled inside the extension package and runs entirely offline. OpenRec does not load or execute any code from external servers.

sections.title: Children's Privacy
sections.content: OpenRec is not directed at children and does not knowingly collect any information from children, in line with its broader practice of not collecting personal information from any user.

sections.title: Your Rights and Control
sections.content: Since your data never leaves your device, you are always in full control of it. You can view, rename, trim, export, back up, restore, or permanently delete any recording at any time from within the extension, and you can remove all OpenRec data instantly by uninstalling the extension or clearing your browser's site/extension data.

sections.title: Changes to This Policy
sections.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in the extension or for other operational, legal, or regulatory reasons. Any changes will be posted with an updated "Last Updated" date above.

sections.title: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy or OpenRec's data practices, please contact us at shyamsunderkanth+openrec@gmail.com.
